instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
What is Term Insurance?
Among the different types of life insurance plans available in the market, a term plan is the basic of them all. It offers unmatched protection and acts as a tool for financial security. Let’s understand what the plan is all about and its different aspects.
What is term insurance?
Term insurance is a pure protection life insurance policy that offers high levels of coverage at very affordable premiums. Term life insurance is the type of life insurance policy in which the sum assured in the plan is paid to the family members in the event of the untimely demise of the policyholder during the term of the policy. On survival till maturity, however, no benefit is usually paid under term plans, with the exception of one type of policy.
Why buy term insurance?
Term insurance is a preferred life insurance product because of the coverage that it offers. Here are some reasons why buying term insurance is important –
Attractive coverage at a low premium
The premiums of term insurance plans are low and affordable. As such, you can choose a high sum assured to secure your family’s financial needs adequately without hurting your pockets in the process.
Protection for financial dependents
Term insurance policy fulfils the primary purpose of securing the family financially in the event of the unfortunate demise of the policyholder. With the guaranteed death benefit, your dependents can manage their lifestyle expenses and also fulfil their financial goals.
Insuring your assets
In the event of the absence of the breadwinner, the dependents in the family might be forced to sell the assets to fund and manage the daily expenses. However, with the death benefit offered by the term plan, the assets remain protected. The benefit can be used for their lifestyle expenses while the assets can remain invested for future financial goals.
Riders
The coverage under term insurance can be enhanced with multiple riders. The riders can augment the term insurance policy by extending its coverage. Riders are very useful and can be brought at a very low additional premium.
What are the features of term insurance?
The salient features of term insurance plans are as follows -
Offers sufficient coverage:The primary objective of a term insurance policy is providing financial security, and therefore the level of security should be sufficient to cover the financial requirements of your family when you are not around. You can opt for your desired sum assured in term insurance policy based on your financial requirements. Usually, it is suggested that your coverage should be 10 to 12 times your annual income to ensure sufficient financial security.
Affordable premiums:A high sum assured can be sustained only if the premiums are low and affordable. Term insurance policies cover the risk of premature death, and therefore, the premiums are low. This allows you to afford a high sum assured at pocket-friendly rates.
Options to add riders:To enable you to extend the coverage, term insurance offers optional riders at an additional premium. Riders are additional coverage that can be brought along with the basic policy to make the policy inclusive. Some popular riders which can be brought with term insurance include accidental death benefit riders, critical illness riders, term riders, terminal illness riders, and accidental death & disablement benefit rider.
Cover for accidental death or disability:Accidents can happen anywhere and anytime. You might require a significant amount of money to meet the medical expenses in the event of an accident. Term insurance also pays the sum assured as a death benefit in case of accidental death of the life insured. Further, you can buy a comprehensive accident benefit rider with term insurance to enjoy extended financial protection for your family by covering yourself for accidental disability or dismemberment.
Easy to buy:In today's times, you can buy a term insurance policy online with just a few clicks. You can also compare various term insurance benefits and customise a term plan with add-ons based on your insurance requirements. Once you select the policy that you want to buy, just fill out the application form, submit relevant documents and pay the premium. The process is pretty simple and hassle-free.
Convenient payout options:Term insurance provides flexible and convenient payout options. You can choose the payout options at the time of purchasing the policy. You can receive the payout either in one lump sum or in regular instalments, which may be fixed or staggered.
Types of Term Insurance Plans in India
There are different types of term insurance plans available in the market. These include the following -
Level Term Insurance:Level term insurance is the basic type of term insurance in which the sum assured selected at the time of buying the policy remains constant throughout the term of the policy. There is no maturity benefit and the sum assured is paid on the death of the insured during the policy tenure.
Increasing Term Insurance:In increasing term insurance, the sum assured increases at regular intervals by a certain percentage or a certain amount. This helps cover your rising financial needs at later stages of life. There’s usually no maturity benefit. In the case of death, the enhanced sum assured available in the year of death is paid.
Decreasing Term Insurance:The exact opposite of an increasing term plan is a decreasing term insurance plan. Under the plan, the sum assured decreases at a predetermined rate. Decreasing term insurance is suited for individuals who have taken a mortgage or a loan and are expecting to pay it off soon. The sum assured usually reduces in tandem with the reduced debt amount. In the case of death, the sum assured available in the year of death is paid.
Term insurance with Return of Premium:Term insurance plans are pure protection policies that do not provide maturity benefits. However, the return of premium term insurance is the exception. Under the plan, the total premium paid is refunded if the insured survives the term of the policy. Thus, if the insured dies during the term, the sum assured is paid, and if he survives, the premiums are refunded back.
How to buy term insurance online?
Buying term insurance online is a simple and quick process. All you need to do is follow the following simple steps:
Estimate financial needs for selecting the right amount of cover:Before selecting a suitable term insurance plan, the first and foremost thing you need to do is assess your financial needs and estimate the amount of coverage that you need. Analyse your source of income, existing financial liabilities, number of dependents, family lifestyle expenses, etc. Moreover, you should also take into consideration your spouse’s and children’s life goals to determine the sum assured. You can use term insurance calculators to find the right coverage amount. Alternatively, as the rule of thumb1, you can opt for coverage which is 10 to 12 times your annual income.
Choose the premium amount you can pay with ease:After selecting the right amount of cover, calculate the amount of premium that you would have to pay. You should ensure an affordable amount so that you can pay with ease. Calculate the premium using the premium calculator and find the affordable rate.
Check the claim settlement ratio of your insurance company:Next, check the claim settlement ratio of the insurance company that you have selected. The claim settlement ratio is the ratio of the claims paid by the company to the number of claims received during a financial year. A high claim settlement ratio shows that the company is reliable to resolve your claim. So, choose an insurer that has a high claim settlement ratio.
Check the riders available for additional coverage:You can extend the coverage through add-ons to meet your financial requirements. Riders are the extra layer of financial cover that can be immensely beneficial for your family during emergencies. Based on your preferences and needs, customize your plan with riders for additional coverage by paying an extra premium.
Finalize the policy and pay the premium:The final step is to pay the premium after finalizing your policy. Simply pay the premium online through any of the secured getaways offered, and the policy would be issued instantly with a confirmation on your registered email address.
Who should buy term insurance?
In a world full of uncertainties, term insurance is the cost-effective financial instrument which offers a large insurance cover for a small premium. While the plan is suitable for everyone who has dependents, the plan proves relevant for the following people:
Young individuals:Insurance rarely features among the priorities of young individuals. However, buying term insurance early has its benefits. You can get a higher level of coverage at lower premiums. Moreover, you can continue the coverage in your later years when you have financial responsibilities.
Married couples:Couples who have recently been married can be benefited from a term insurance policy as it would provide financial support to their spouse if they predecease them. A term insurance policy is a significant part of good financial planning for young couples who want to live life with financial independence.
New parents:A term insurance policy provides new parents with peace of mind that their child will be taken care of even if something happens to them. New parents should buy a term insurance policy to provide a safety net for their children in their absence.
Couples with older children:Couples with older children generally have significant financial milestones and goals such as funding higher education for their children and creating a corpus for their marriage. With the increasing cost of higher education and costly weddings, a term insurance policy offering sufficient coverage demonstrates financial prudence on the part of parents.
Individuals nearing their retirement:When an individual is near their retirement, liabilities are generally taken care of. With children settled, and debts paid, it is time for you to enjoy your life after retirement. However, an unexpected contingency might disrupt your planned retirement. A term plan helps prevent this disruption as you leave behind financial assistance for your spouse if you are not around.
How much life cover do I need?
Buying a term plan with optimal coverage is essential for complete financial security. Usually, a multiple of your annual income can be the easiest way to determine the right amount of coverage. However, though the thumb rule states 10 to 12 times the annual income, the multiple depends on your age.
So, here are the age-specific multiples based on which you should decide how much coverage you would need –
This multiple is based on the assumption that as you age, your liabilities and financial goals reduce. As such, the coverage requirement goes down.
Conclusion
Understand the meaning of the term insurance plan and know its benefits. Opt for a policy in your portfolio to provide financial security for your family. Death is uncertain but you can prepare for its financial repercussions with the help of a term insurance plan. So, buy the plan with a suitable sum assured and take care of your family’s needs even when you are not around.
Frequently Asked Questions
What do you mean by term insurance premium?
The term insurance premium is the amount of money that an individual pays to the insurance company for the financial coverage in the term insurance policy. Term insurance premiums may be paid quarterly, monthly, annually, or semi-annually.
What do you mean by term insurance rider?
Riders are the add-on benefits that are attached to the basic term insurance plan to enhance its coverage. Term insurance riders can be purchased at a nominal premium rate. In essence, the riders help to maximize the benefits of your term insurance cover. You can customize the term plan through riders according to your insurance requirements. Common riders available with term plans include the following
Accidental Death Benefit Rider
Critical Illness Rider
Term Rider
Waiver of Premium Rider
How much term insurance do I need?
The thumb rule states that the sum assured in the term insurance policy should be at least 10 to 12 times the annual income of the insured. But you should calculate the coverage of term insurance based on your need and the amount of premium you can afford to pay. You can also use different methods to calculate the right coverage amount or the online term insurance calculator.
Does term insurance offer tax benefits?
Term insurance plans help you to save tax and reduce your tax burden. Tax benefits under term insurance include the following:
Under Section 80C of the Income Tax Act- You can claim a deduction on the premium paid for term insurance up to 1.5 Lakh under section 80C of the Income Tax Act 1961.
Under Section 80D of the Income Tax Act- You can also avail of tax deduction under section 80D for up to Rs. 25000 if you buy a critical illness rider.
The death benefit is always tax-free
Under section 10 (10D) of the Income Tax Act- The maturity benefit received under return of premium plans also qualifies for a tax exemption under this section.
What affects the premiums of a term insurance plan?
The insurance provider considers various factors before deciding the premium of your term insurance policy. The main factors that affect the premium include your age, your gender, chosen sum assured, medical history, premium payment tenure, lifestyle, geographical location, BMI index, and occupation.
Does term insurance cover critical illness?
No, the coverage for critical illness is not inbuilt under the policy. You do get a death benefit in the case of death due to the illness. However, being diagnosed is not usually covered. However, many term plans offer the optional critical illness rider. You can opt for the rider and get a lump sum benefit even when you get diagnosed with any of the illness covered by the critical illness rider.
Can I add a rider to my existing term insurance policy?
Yes, you can add riders to your existing term insurance policy at the time of renewing it. The rider's additional premium cost would be added to the premium and your aggregate premium would be adjusted accordingly.
What documents are required to buy term insurance plans?
The documents required to be submitted along with the application form to buy a term insurance plan are as follows
A proof of identity
Your recent photograph
Valid address proof such as an Aadhaar Card, driving license passbook, or any other valid address proof
Valid Age proof such as birth certificate, Aadhaar card, etc.
Valid income proof such as the last three month’s salary slip, Form 16, bank statement of the account in which the salary is credited, or any other valid address proof
What are the minimum age and maximum age criteria to buy a term insurance policy?
The minimum age to buy a term insurance plan is generally 18 years and the maximum age is 65 years. However, the minimum and maximum age criteria for term insurance policies vary among different types of term insurance and different insurance providers.
What are the common exclusions under the term insurance policy?
Term insurance plans cover premature death of the life insured. However, some events are not covered in term insurance plans commonly known as exclusions. Some of the common exclusions of term insurance include the following:
Death due to pre-existing medical illness not disclosed when buying the plan
Death due to suicide or other self-inflicted injuries
Death due to driving under influence of alcohol or drugs
Death due to participating in adventurous or illegal/ criminal activities
Death due to conditions regarding lifestyle such as smoking habits, excessive intake of alcohol, etc.
Ref:
https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/wealth/insure/life-insurance/what-to-look-for-in-a-term-life-insurance-policy/articleshow/89196568.cms
